title: Backing Up Data
subtitle: Introduction to USB backup process. Keep your files safe!
overview: In this vital digital literacy session, students learn the importance of preventing data loss. They explore what a backup is, discover the 3-2-1 backup rule, and master how to safely copy, transfer, and eject files using a physical USB flash drive.

images._note: Add photos from the actual session — component photos, activity shots, student experiments, whiteboard diagrams.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Diagram illustrating data duplication from computer to external drive
gallery.caption: Data Backup: Creating an exact duplicate copy to protect against accidents

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Photo of the USB Flash Drives distributed to students
gallery.caption: USB Flash Drive — Our pocket-sized data vault!

gallery.caption: Dragging and dropping files into the USB drive folder

gallery.caption: Crucial Step: Always safely eject before pulling out the drive!

keyTopics: What is Data Loss? — When original files get accidentally deleted, corrupted by viruses, or lost due to computer hardware failure
The Data Backup Concept — Making an identical copy of your work and storing it in a completely separate location
Photocopy Analogy — A backup is like photocopying your school homework notebook so if the original gets lost, you do not have to rewrite everything
USB Flash Drive — A removable storage device that uses flash memory to store digital data without needing electricity
Copy vs. Move — 'Copying' duplicates the file (leaves original behind ✅), while 'Moving' cuts and transfers it (erases original from computer ❌)
Safely Ejecting — Informing the computer operating system to finish writing lingering memory tasks before physical removal to prevent data corruption

activity.title: The USB Time Capsule Mission
parts: USB Flash Drive (FAT32 or NTFS formatted)
steps: Create a new folder on the computer desktop and name it 'My_Time_Capsule'
Put 3 sample files inside the folder (e.g., a drawing or written document)
Locate the physical USB port on your machine and gently insert the USB flash drive
Open File Explorer, right-click your 'My_Time_Capsule' folder, and select 'Copy'
Navigate inside the USB drive folder, right-click an empty space, and select 'Paste'
Verify the files exist in both locations, right-click the USB drive icon, select 'Eject', and safely unplug it!

options: Deleting old files to free up computer space
An identical copy of your files stored in a separate location
Changing the file names to make them look nice
Locking your computer with a strong password
quiz.explanation: A backup creates a duplicate copy of your data to keep it safe.

quiz.q: What is the safest way to remove a USB flash drive from a computer?
quiz.explanation: Always use the 'Safely Remove Hardware' option to prevent data corruption.
'Copy' creates a duplicate while 'Move' transfers the original.

quiz.q: What is the best way to keep your files safe from computer crashes?
quiz.explanation: Regular backups ensure you don't lose important data if something goes wrong.

quiz.q: What is a USB flash drive primarily used for?
quiz.explanation: USB flash drives are designed for portable data storage and transfer.
